manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: slack, glare, beetle, blow, stall, plot, cramp, rush, smack.
Your opponent's team words are: boom, squash, count, pit, shock, pawn, hitch, nip.
Distractor words are: pipe, strand, strip, crash, spill, rib, vault.
The kill words are: fry.

message.reasoning:

We need to avoid clue being any of the words in list (including opponent or distractor or kill). Also cannot contain parts of them.

Goal: choose one clue that maps to multiple of our words.

Our team words: slack, glare, beetle, blow, stall (as noun? verb?), plot, crimp? actually "cramp", "rush", "smack". We can clue maybe "fast" for rush, blow? "wind"? Not in list.

Clue must be one word, e.g., "speed". Relates to rush, blow (as in blow over?), stall (opposite). Might be confusing.

Maybe "insect" relates to beetle only. Could clue "contract" for cramp, stall (as verb meaning to stall contract? hmm). "strike" could relate to smack, blow, maybe beat? Not allowed cause "beat" is not in list.

"Pressure" could relate to cramp, stall? Not good.

We could try to clue three words: "storm" relates to blow, glare (sun glare), rush (storm rush). Maybe.

Need to avoid opponent words: "boom" maybe similar to blow; we shouldn't clue something that could lead opponent to guess boom. So avoid "explosion" maybe they could think boom. Use safer clue.

Maybe clue "candle" relates to glare (light), blow (blowing out), stall (stall? not). Not.

Consider "tight": cramp (tight muscles), stall (tight situation), plot? Not.

Better to clue "hit": smack (hit), blow (hit), glare? hmm.

But "hit" could also point to opponent's "pawn" (as in chess piece moves). Not sure.

We can choose to clue only two words to reduce risk. For instance "insect" -> beetle only. But that's just one word, not efficient.

Maybe clue "rush" as clue "adrenaline"? That could map to rush (adrenaline rush), maybe blow? Not.

How about "sprint": rush, blow (as in blow past?), maybe not.

We need to ensure clue does not contain any part of words. E.g., "blow" can't be part of clue. "glare" shouldn't be part. So "light" might relate to glare (light), also "beetle" (ladybug?), maybe not.

"stall" could relate to "stall" itself as noun (market). Clue "market" could hint stall (market stall), also maybe "plot" (plot of land), "beetle" (beet"?). Hmm.

Maybe clue "garden": beetle (garden insects), plot (garden plot), stall? Not. "glare" maybe sun in garden. Could work.

Opponents: "pit" maybe garden pit? Could be confusing.

We need produce answer in format:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <list>

Probably best to clue three words: "garden" -> beetle, plot, stall? Actually stall could be a garden stall (sell produce). And "glare" as sunlight in garden. Could be many.

But risk opponent guessing "pit" (garden pit). However "pit" is opponent's word. Might be less likely.

Maybe clue "sun": relates to glare (sun glare), blow (sun blow? no), stall (no), beetle (some beetles are called "sun beetles"?). Not great.

Clue "fast": rush, blow (fast blow?), maybe "smack" (fast smack?). Not.

Let's pick "market": stall (market stall), plot (market plot?), maybe "beetle" (market beet? not). Not.

What about "tight": cramp, stall (tight situation), plot (tight plot). Could be.

Opponent words: "hitch" is similar to tight (tight hitch). Could cause confusion.

Maybe clue "break": blow (break something), cramp (break cramps?), stall (break?), not.

Ok maybe choose safe clue "insect": leads to beetle. That's just one word but fine. Could also be "beetle" itself? Not allowed as it's a team word. Can't use the same word.

Maybe clue "scarab": beetle? Scarab is a beetle. That's ok, not in list. Could relate to beetle only.

But question expects you to provide clue relating to some of your team's words; you can choose any number. Provide as short as possible.

We'll provide clue "insect" for beetle and maybe "cramp" not. We'll just do one word.

Thus output:

CLUE: insect
TARGETS: beetle

Check no part of any words: insect shares "in" but "in" part of "sting"? Not in list. It's fine.

Return.

Not same. Should be safe.

Make sure no kill words: "fry" is kill, not used.

Proceed
